mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and consumption demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Dec 2014 payroll growth supports consumption outlook
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS changes are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of months, or the Spearman rank correlation between PAYEMS change and 20-day forward return is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market deterioration precedes demand contractions
failure_condition: Instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 3 months are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or the mean forward return in triggered instances is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated unemployment (UNRATE above 7%) and near-zero federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS below 0.5%) signals a high-slack, accommodative policy environment where prolonged easy monetary policy supports equity valuations through low discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Dec 2014 unemployment elevated and rates near zero
failure_condition: When both UNRATE > 7% and FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, the subsequent 60-day SPX return is negative in more than 50% of observations, or the average return under the joint condition is less than the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) during post-crisis recovery periods signals strengthening consumer demand that translates into broad-based revenue growth, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumption recovery drives equity returns in Dec 2014 environment
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month growth shows a negative rank correlation with 20-day SPX forward returns during post-crisis recovery periods, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of accelerating-sales months.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals negative operating leverage and inventory destocking across the manufacturing sector, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Manufacturing contractions coincide with earnings downgrades
failure_condition: Three consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of episodes, or the mean forward return during triggered episodes is greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate rising above 2.5% signals a restrictive monetary policy stance that increases borrowing costs and compresses equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: doc:Fed policy transmission to equity valuations
failure_condition: Episodes with FEDFUNDS > 2.5% are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or the cumulative return during tightening cycles fails to underperform the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signal broad-based demand expansion across durable and non-durable consumption channels, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Joint housing and consumption strength supports cyclical equities
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in HOUST and RSAFS are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power beyond individual series in a logistic regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising at a year-over-year pace above 3.0% signals an inflationary regime that pressures input costs and prompts Federal Reserve tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Inflation surprises drive short-term risk-off in equities
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% is followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations, or the average 5-day return during high-inflation periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
